title: Map Your Habit Engine: Build a System of Connected Behaviors That Drive Real Goals

sections.title: Audit Your Current Habits
sections.prompt: List 5–7 habits you practice regularly. For each, mark whether it's Foundation (sleep, movement, nutrition), Work (deep focus, iteration), or Reflection (journaling, review). Then ask: is this habit isolated, or does it connect to another habit on your list? Which ones feel disconnected?

sections.title: Find Your Momentum Points
sections.prompt: Pick one Work habit you care about. Trace backward: which Foundation habit creates the conditions for it? Trace forward: how could its output feed into a Reflection habit? Write one specific momentum connection (example: 'Morning meditation → sharper thinking during deep work → clarity in evening review').

sections.title: Spot Your Discipline Drains
sections.prompt: Which 2–3 habits feel like pure willpower to maintain? For each ask: Is this connected to something I deeply care about? Or does it sit alone? What would need to change for this to feel like it *makes sense* instead of feels forced? Where is discipline covering for poor architecture?

sections.title: Design Your Feedback Loop
sections.prompt: An engine works because you can *see* it working. Pick one goal. What Reflection habit will show you if your Foundation and Work habits are actually supporting it? (Not just logging meditation minutes, but: are you clearer? Faster? More focused?) Design one simple feedback loop that proves the system matters.

sections.title: Plan Your First Iteration
sections.prompt: Pick ONE habit that feels disconnected or forced. Remove it, reconnect it to something you care about, or restructure when/how you do it. What's the smallest change you can test this week? The system evolves — what's your first adjustment?

cta: What does your habit engine look like right now? Which connection surprised you in this worksheet? Reply with one habit link you're building or rebuilding this week — I'm genuinely curious how you're structuring your system.
